H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong (VOL001). Largest HEALING sample; broad positive distribution (66% positive). Qualitative: 29 respondents (16 self, 6 support, 5 client, 2 other); 79% improvement, settle 5-10 min, music loved (9) / liked (10), rhythm very settling (8) / balanced (10). YouTube: 30 views (series high), no retention. AutoGluon overstim prediction 6.859 (Δ-0.29) — model underestimates. Panel convergent: anchor protocol with strongest qualitative convergence.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: The most reliable overstimulation protocol — strong improvement, calming rhythm, music loved. Still flying under the radar on YouTube.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong (VOL001). Mid-range outcomes, peak at 7-8 (65% positive). Qualitative: 21 respondents (11 self, 5 client, 3 support, 2 other); 76% improvement but music polarized (10 liked, 6 neutral), settle 10 min for half. YouTube: 5 views. Drift 0.780. Avg_view_percentage 10% suggests pre-entrainment drop-off. Panel recommends hold-phase extension.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Works well for overstimulation but listeners may drop off before the full effect kicks in. Music reactions are polarized.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Compact distribution, strong central tendency (64% positive). Qualitative: 23 respondents (15 self, 4 support, 3 other, 1 client); 78% improvement, rhythm balanced (10) / very calming (10), activity includes sleep attempts. YouTube: 8 views. Drift 0.784. Panel notes utility in transition contexts (sleep/work boundaries).
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Consistent help for overstimulation, especially when transitioning to sleep or winding down from work. Balanced, calming rhythm.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong (VOL001 only). Distribution shows broad upper-range concentration with positive skew (63% positive). Qualitative: 23 respondents (13 self, 6 client, 4 support); 68% report moderate-to-significant improvement, settle mode ~10 min, rhythm rated balanced/comfortable (13/23). YouTube: 7 views, no retention data — high efficacy undiscovered. AutoGluon predicts anxiety zone 7.838 (Δ+0.62), cross-buckets to X-ME-L1/X-MD-L1. Panel notes adviser-driven traffic (5/67) correlates with stronger outcomes.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners feel noticeably calmer within ten minutes, and the rhythm feels comfortable. It works well but not enough people have found it yet.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Tighter distribution centered at 7-8 with bimodal hint at 6 and 8 (67% positive). Qualitative: 20 respondents (12 self, 6 client, 2 support); 55% moderate/noticeable improvement, mixed music opinion (9 liked, 5 neutral), settle 5-20 min. YouTube: 6 views, no retention. Cross-run drift high (0.652) — analytical instability across runs. No AutoGluon uplift. Panel divided on whether groove style in VOL001 dampens entrainment for subgroup.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: A steady option for anxiety — some feel better quickly, others take longer. Reactions to the music are mixed.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Moderate peak at 7-8 with notable spread (61% positive). Qualitative: 20 respondents (11 self, 6 support, 2 client, 1 other); 75% noticeable-or-better improvement, longer settle (10+ min for 10/20), rhythm very calming (10/20). YouTube: 4 views (lowest in AX series). Drift high (0.695). AutoGluon anxiety prediction 7.84 consistent. Low discoverability limits subgroup analysis.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: People report solid improvement, though it takes a bit longer to settle in. The rhythm feels very calming.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Expected low mean for crisis arrival; 53% positive structurally acceptable. Qualitative: 24 respondents (11 self, 7 client, 5 support, 1 other); 68% significant/noticeable improvement, rapid settle (5 min for 8, 10 min for 5), rhythm balanced/comforting (11/23). YouTube: 6 views. AutoGluon crisis prediction 7.955 favors H-CR-L2 — suggests H-CR-L1 underperforms relative to alternative. Panel notes strong qualitative/outcome divergence.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Even in crisis moments, many feel significant relief within minutes. The rhythm feels balancing and the music is well-liked.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Slightly above H-CR-L1 (55% positive). Qualitative: 16 respondents (9 self, 3 client, 2 support, 2 other); moderate/significant improvement dominant, mixed music (5 loved, 6 neutral), rhythm very settling (8/16). YouTube: 4 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 (top crisis) — large efficacy/reach gap. Drift 0.662. Cross-buckets to X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2. Panel convergent: high-potential protocol severely underexposed.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Quick relief is common and the rhythm feels very settling. Music opinions vary — some love it, others find it just okay.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Matches H-CR-L2 mean with higher variance (52% positive). Qualitative: 19 respondents (10 self, 4 support, 3 other, 2 client); 63% moderate-or-better, rapid settle (immediate for 7, 5 min for 5), music mixed (7 liked, 4 not liked), rhythm very settling (12/19). YouTube: 5 views. Drift 0.711. No AutoGluon uplift. Panel notes immediate settle signal but music polarity risk.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Fastest settle of the crisis protocols — many feel better almost immediately. Music taste splits sharply though.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong (VOL001). Highest sleep mean, wide spread (65% positive). Qualitative: 15 respondents (13 self, 1 other, 1 support); 80% improvement, fast settle (≤5 min for 8), music liked (10) / loved (3), rhythm balanced (7) / very calming (4). YouTube: 4 views. AutoGluon sleep prediction 7.539 (Δ+0.35) — strong alignment. Drift 0.689. Panel: anchor sleep protocol with stable model convergence.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Fast-acting sleep aid — most feel better within five minutes. Music and rhythm both get high marks.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Tighter distribution than L1 (67% positive). Qualitative: 15 respondents (6 client, 5 support, 4 self); weaker improvement (60% moderate+), 'too busy' rhythm feedback (3/15), settle 10-20 min. YouTube: 5 views. Drift 0.802 (borderline). Panel divided: some see groove complexity issue, others see client-population scale difference.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Helps with sleep but takes longer to settle. A few listeners found the rhythm a bit busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Near-identical mean to L2, slightly less consistency (64% positive). Qualitative: 22 respondents (12 self, 7 support, 3 client); 77% improvement, rhythm balanced (9) / very settling (7), settle 5-10 min. YouTube: 3 views (series low). Drift 0.785. Panel suggests deeper sleep-focused refinement for L3 variant.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Reliable sleep support with balanced rhythm. Works across different listener types — self, caregivers, clients.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Lowest FC series, long left tail (56% positive). Qualitative: 23 respondents (12 self, 5 support, 4 client, 2 other); 76% improvement (qualitative > outcome), 'slightly busy' rhythm (9/23). YouTube: 5 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (Δ+1.27), cross-buckets to X-ME-L1 — clear cross-zone influence signal. Panel: outcome score may underrepresent benefit; Hz parameter review warranted.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Lowest focus scores but listeners still report improvement. Some find the rhythm slightly busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Strong rebound from L1 (66% positive). Qualitative: 17 respondents (9 self, 4 support, 3 client, 1 other); mixed music (6 neutral), rhythm comfort high. YouTube: 4 views. Drift 0.754. No AutoGluon elevation. Panel: mid-tier performer without breakout potential; groove may suit maintenance over focus.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Solid focus support with comfortable rhythm. Music reactions mixed but outcomes consistent.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Highest THRIVING mean, dramatic 71% positive. Qualitative: 29 respondents (13 self, 9 support, 4 client, 3 other); 69% significant improvement, music loved (8), rhythm balanced (15). YouTube: 15 views (FC series high). AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (Δ+0.10), selects as top focus protocol — strong convergence. Panel convergent: L4 variant exploration priority.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Standout focus protocol — strong improvement, loved music, balanced rhythm. Highest YouTube reach in the series.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Moderate outcomes, bimodal hint (53% positive). Qualitative: 21 respondents (13 self, 5 support, 2 other, 1 client); 47% slight improvement only, music positive, rhythm not strongly impactful. YouTube: 2 views (near absent). Drift 0.820. No AutoGluon uplift. Panel: lower comparative efficacy in creative flow HOLD design.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Modest creative flow support. Many only feel slight improvement. Rhythm doesn't stand out as impactful.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Stable outcomes around 7, tight distribution (58% positive). Qualitative: 15 respondents (10 self, 3 client, 2 support); 80% significant/moderate improvement, music loved by half. YouTube: 2 views. Drift 0.768. No AutoGluon uplift. Panel notes under-the-radar efficacy with strong qualitative signal.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Stronger creative flow feel — most report meaningful improvement. Half the listeners loved the music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing (VOL001). Highest creative flow mean, compact spread (67% positive). Qualitative: 25 respondents (10 self, 8 support, 5 client, 2 other); 72% significant/noticeable, music split (9 liked, 7 neutral), rhythm balanced (14). YouTube: 7 views. Drift 0.767. Panel: consider L4 development if pattern holds — possible ceiling effect with n=18.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Best creative flow scores so far. Compact, consistent results. Music opinions split but rhythm feels balanced.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Compact distribution, solid outcomes (64% positive). Qualitative: 23 respondents (11 self, 6 client, 6 support); 65% improvement, slow settle (20 min for 9), rhythm balanced (12). YouTube: 3 views. Drift 0.739. AutoGluon maintenance prediction 7.693 routes to X-ME-L1 — suggests HOLD design misalignment. Panel: settle-time design may need acceleration.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Steady maintenance support but takes longer to settle in (around 20 minutes). Rhythm feels balanced.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). High variance, bimodal pull toward 5 and 7 (59% positive). Qualitative: 16 respondents (7 self, 7 client, 2 support); 68% improvement but 'slightly busy' rhythm (7/16). YouTube: 4 views. Drift 0.785. No model uplift. Panel: possible groove misfit in VOL001 for maintenance HOLD.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed results — some feel good, others less so. Rhythm can feel slightly busy for a maintenance protocol.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing (VOL001). Small n, solid outcomes (71% positive). Qualitative: 19 respondents (15 self, 3 support, 1 other); 79% improvement but 4 did not settle, rhythm divided. YouTube: 3 views. Drift 0.743. Panel: too early to assess; potential for settle-time refinement.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Promising but early data. Most improve but a subset don't settle at all. Needs more listeners.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Modest mean but unique hub signal — appears in 5/9 cross-buckets (crisis, focus, maintenance, motivation, low). Qualitative: 22 respondents (10 self, 6 client, 5 support, 1 other); 68% improvement, slow settle (20 min for 6), music loved (10). YouTube: 8 views. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 for low zone (Δ+1.49) — strongest prediction in set. Panel convergent: investigate as bridge protocol candidate.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Unique 'bridge' signal — model recommends it for five different states. Listeners improve but settle slowly. Highest model prediction.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Nearly identical to L1 (64% positive). Qualitative: 15 respondents (8 self, 3 support, 3 client, 1 other); 80% improvement, music liked (7) / loved (6), settle variable. YouTube: 8 views. Drift second-worst (0.532). Appears in 4 cross-buckets. Panel: reinforces bridge hypothesis; may stabilize with more data.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Nearly identical to L1 with faster settle for some. Reinforces the bridge-protocol idea. Data still stabilizing.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing (VOL001). Dip in mean (56% positive). Qualitative: 25 respondents (12 self, 7 client, 6 support); 60% improvement, 5 report no effect, rhythm split. YouTube: 4 views. Drift high (0.627). No AutoGluon elevation. Panel: L1/L2 remain stronger ASCENT candidates; L3 may need redesign.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Weaker than L1/L2. Some feel no effect. Rhythm opinions split. L1 and L2 remain the stronger mood-energy options.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Low mean, high spread for ASCENT arc (45% positive). Qualitative: 17 respondents (10 self, 4 client, 2 support, 1 other); 68% improvement (qualitative > outcome), settle ~5 min. YouTube: 5 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 for motivation (Δ+1.52), routes to X-MD-L1 — strong model support despite modest observed. Panel: largest prediction gap in dataset; ASCENT design may need gradual onset.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation support shows promise in feedback but scores lag behind. Works fast (5 min settle) but model expects much more.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing (VOL001). Dip from L1, slow settle (20 min for 5/21) (43% positive). Qualitative: 21 respondents (11 self, 7 support, 2 client, 1 other); music loved (9), rhythm slightly busy (6). YouTube: 7 views. Drift extreme (0.585) — highest instability after X-MD-L3. No AutoGluon elevation. Panel: L2 groove may disrupt ascending arc; tempo refinement needed.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Dips below L1. Slow settle for some, rhythm slightly busy. Music loved though. Data unstable across runs.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing (VOL001). Sharp mean improvement with high variance (62% positive). Qualitative: 17 respondents (12 self, 2 support, 2 client, 1 other); moderate/significant improvement, rhythm balanced (8) / slightly busy (4). YouTube: 7 views. Drift worst in set (0.507). AutoGluon does not predict high. Panel: outlier effect possible; monitor closely — unstable or evolving response pattern.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Surprising jump in mean score but high variance and unstable patterns. Early data — watch this one.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Mid-range, stable peak at 7 (54% positive). Qualitative: 25 respondents (13 self, 7 support, 4 client, 1 other); 12 report worsening — concerning signal, rhythm well-received. YouTube: 8 views. Drift 0.655. AutoGluon predicts 7.538, favors X-MC-L2 — L1 may not be optimal mindclarity DESCENT. Panel: worsening reports warrant investigation.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mixed mind-clarity results — some report worsening. Rhythm is well-liked but outcomes are inconsistent.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing (VOL001). Slightly better than L1 (56% positive). Qualitative: 20 respondents (16 self, 4 support); 70% significant/moderate improvement, rhythm highly calming (12/20). YouTube: 12 views (TRANSFORMING high). AutoGluon selects as top mindclarity (7.538) — alignment supports routing elevation. Panel: strongest MC candidate despite low n.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Best mind-clarity protocol so far. Strong improvement reports, very calming rhythm. Still early data.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing (VOL001). Lowest TRANSFORMING mean, extreme variance (50% positive). Qualitative: 17 respondents (7 self, 7 client, 3 support); 4 report 'too busy' rhythm, music neutral (5). YouTube: 8 views. No AutoGluon uplift. Panel convergent: structural review needed — DESCENT/ASCENT arc mismatch at L3 intensity with bimodal risk.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Lowest scores in the series with high variability. Several listeners found the rhythm too busy. Needs review.

sphere_commentary.overview:
All three series demonstrate clinically meaningful efficacy with distinct profiles: HEALING delivers consistent regulation, THRIVING peaks in cognitive optimization, and TRANSFORMING shows expected variance for ascending-arc designs.
• HEALING protocols cluster near 7.0 with strong confidence tiers and predictable DESCENT entrainment
• THRIVING achieves highest observed mean (T-FC-L3 at 7.63) with HOLD-phase stabilization
• TRANSFORMING carries widest spread (std=1.87) and lowest mean (6.59), consistent with low-baseline arrival states
• AutoGluon identifies avg_view_percentage and avg_view_duration as top predictors, yet YouTube reach remains minimal across all protocols
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series performance aligns with design intent, though TRANSFORMING's ASCENT protocols show structural underperformance relative to model predictions.
• HEALING: High consistency across 12 protocols (mean 6.98, std 1.77); Over-stimulation and Sleep entries show strongest qualitative convergence
• THRIVING: Focus & Clarity emerges as standout zone (T-FC-L3 7.63); Creative Flow and Maintenance show moderate efficacy with tighter distributions
• TRANSFORMING: Motivation & Drive ASCENT protocols underperform predictions (X-MD-L1 predicted 7.88 vs observed 6.36); Mood & Energy shows bridge-protocol signal via X-ME-L1 cross-bucket dominance
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols diverge from series expectations and model predictions, suggesting population mismatch or design friction.
• X-MC-L3: Lowest mean (5.70), highest variance (std=2.45), 50% positive rate — potential DESCENT/ASCENT arc mismatch at L3 intensity
• X-MD-L1/L2: Observed means (6.36, 6.21) far below AutoGluon motivation prediction (7.88) despite qualitative improvement reports
• H-CR-L2: Model predicts 7.955 for crisis zone but observed mean is 6.52 — large efficacy/reach gap
• X-ME-L1: Appears in 5/9 cross-bucket recommendations yet observed mean (6.74) lags low-zone prediction (8.23) — possible routing misalignment
• H-AX-L1: Strong qualitative reports but 0.6-point gap to AutoGluon anxiety prediction (7.84 vs 7.22)
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Convergent signals point to routing realignment, variant development, and engagement timing adjustments.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63): Create L4 variant to test ceiling effects; strongest convergence of observed, qualitative, and predicted signals
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L1 (n=39): Investigate as bridge protocol — appears in 5 cross-buckets with highest AutoGluon low-zone prediction (8.23)
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-CR-L2 (n=33): Increase promotional reach; high predicted efficacy (7.955) but minimal views (4) suggests underutilization
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-AX-L1 (n=67): Adjust adviser routing to leverage strong qualitative signal despite prediction gap
• X-MC-L3 (n=10): Structural review of L3 DESCENT arc; bimodal risk with 'too busy' rhythm feedback (4/17)
• X-MD-L3 (n=13): Investigate tempo refinement; highest cross-run drift (0.507) and 'slightly busy' rhythm signal
• H-OS-L2 (n=51): Extend hold phase; low avg_view_percentage (10%) despite strong outcomes suggests pre-entrainment drop-off
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Cross-run drift is pervasive and severe, particularly in TRANSFORMING protocols, indicating unstable response patterns or evolving analytical framing.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 29 of 30 protocols show similarity < 0.85; most severe in X-MD-L3 (0.507), X-ME-L2 (0.532), X-MD-L2 (0.585) — TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols dominate drift rankings
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient embedding data for cluster analysis — only earliest and latest runs available
• Confidence note: Drift metrics are reliable but limited to two timepoints; semantic clustering requires more runs and protocol coverage

Qualitative: 21 respondents (13 self, 5 support, 2 other, 1 client); 47% slight improvement only, music positive, rhythm not strongly impactful. YouTube: 2 views (near absent). Drift 0.820. No AutoGluon uplift. Panel: lower comparative efficacy in creative flow HOLD design.
by_protocol.T-CF-L2: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Stable outcomes around 7, tight distribution (58% positive). Qualitative: 15 respondents (10 self, 3 client, 2 support); 80% significant/moderate improvement, music loved by half. YouTube: 2 views. Drift 0.768. No AutoGluon uplift. Panel notes under-the-radar efficacy with strong qualitative signal.
by_protocol.T-CF-L3: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing (VOL001). Highest creative flow mean, compact spread (67% positive). Qualitative: 25 respondents (10 self, 8 support, 5 client, 2 other); 72% significant/noticeable, music split (9 liked, 7 neutral), rhythm balanced (14). YouTube: 7 views. Drift 0.767. Panel: consider L4 development if pattern holds — possible ceiling effect with n=18.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Lowest FC series, long left tail (56% positive). Qualitative: 23 respondents (12 self, 5 support, 4 client, 2 other); 76% improvement (qualitative > outcome), 'slightly busy' rhythm (9/23). YouTube: 5 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (Δ+1.27), cross-buckets to X-ME-L1 — clear cross-zone influence signal. Panel: outcome score may underrepresent benefit; Hz parameter review warranted.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Strong rebound from L1 (66% positive). Qualitative: 17 respondents (9 self, 4 support, 3 client, 1 other); mixed music (6 neutral), rhythm comfort high. YouTube: 4 views. Drift 0.754. No AutoGluon elevation. Panel: mid-tier performer without breakout potential; groove may suit maintenance over focus.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Highest THRIVING mean, dramatic 71% positive. Qualitative: 29 respondents (13 self, 9 support, 4 client, 3 other); 69% significant improvement, music loved (8), rhythm balanced (15). YouTube: 15 views (FC series high). AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (Δ+0.10), selects as top focus protocol — strong convergence. Panel convergent: L4 variant exploration priority.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Compact distribution, solid outcomes (64% positive). Qualitative: 23 respondents (11 self, 6 client, 6 support); 65% improvement, slow settle (20 min for 9), rhythm balanced (12). YouTube: 3 views. Drift 0.739. AutoGluon maintenance prediction 7.693 routes to X-ME-L1 — suggests HOLD design misalignment. Panel: settle-time design may need acceleration.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). High variance, bimodal pull toward 5 and 7 (59% positive). Qualitative: 16 respondents (7 self, 7 client, 2 support); 68% improvement but 'slightly busy' rhythm (7/16). YouTube: 4 views. Drift 0.785. No model uplift. Panel: possible groove misfit in VOL001 for maintenance HOLD.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing (VOL001). Small n, solid outcomes (71% positive). Qualitative: 19 respondents (15 self, 3 support, 1 other); 79% improvement but 4 did not settle, rhythm divided. YouTube: 3 views. Drift 0.743. Panel: too early to assess; potential for settle-time refinement.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Mid-range, stable peak at 7 (54% positive). Qualitative: 25 respondents (13 self, 7 support, 4 client, 1 other); 12 report worsening — concerning signal, rhythm well-received. YouTube: 8 views. Drift 0.655. AutoGluon predicts 7.538, favors X-MC-L2 — L1 may not be optimal mindclarity DESCENT. Panel: worsening reports warrant investigation.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing (VOL001). Slightly better than L1 (56% positive). Qualitative: 20 respondents (16 self, 4 support); 70% significant/moderate improvement, rhythm highly calming (12/20). YouTube: 12 views (TRANSFORMING high). AutoGluon selects as top mindclarity (7.538) — alignment supports routing elevation. Panel: strongest MC candidate despite low n.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing (VOL001). Lowest TRANSFORMING mean, extreme variance (50% positive). Qualitative: 17 respondents (7 self, 7 client, 3 support); 4 report 'too busy' rhythm, music neutral (5). YouTube: 8 views. No AutoGluon uplift. Panel convergent: structural review needed — DESCENT/ASCENT arc mismatch at L3 intensity with bimodal risk.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Low mean, high spread for ASCENT arc (45% positive). Qualitative: 17 respondents (10 self, 4 client, 2 support, 1 other); 68% improvement (qualitative > outcome), settle ~5 min. YouTube: 5 views. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 for motivation (Δ+1.52), routes to X-MD-L1 — strong model support despite modest observed. Panel: largest prediction gap in dataset; ASCENT design may need gradual onset.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing (VOL001). Dip from L1, slow settle (20 min for 5/21) (43% positive). Qualitative: 21 respondents (11 self, 7 support, 2 client, 1 other); music loved (9), rhythm slightly busy (6). YouTube: 7 views. Drift extreme (0.585) — highest instability after X-MD-L3. No AutoGluon elevation. Panel: L2 groove may disrupt ascending arc; tempo refinement needed.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing (VOL001). Sharp mean improvement with high variance (62% positive). Qualitative: 17 respondents (12 self, 2 support, 2 client, 1 other); moderate/significant improvement, rhythm balanced (8) / slightly busy (4). YouTube: 7 views. Drift worst in set (0.507). AutoGluon does not predict high. Panel: outlier effect possible; monitor closely — unstable or evolving response pattern.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Modest mean but unique hub signal — appears in 5/9 cross-buckets (crisis, focus, maintenance, motivation, low). Qualitative: 22 respondents (10 self, 6 client, 5 support, 1 other); 68% improvement, slow settle (20 min for 6), music loved (10). YouTube: 8 views. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 for low zone (Δ+1.49) — strongest prediction in set. Panel convergent: investigate as bridge protocol candidate.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful (VOL001). Nearly identical to L1 (64% positive). Qualitative: 15 respondents (8 self, 3 support, 3 client, 1 other); 80% improvement, music liked (7) / loved (6), settle variable. YouTube: 8 views. Drift second-worst (0.532). Appears in 4 cross-buckets. Panel: reinforces bridge hypothesis; may stabilize with more data.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing (VOL001). Dip in mean (56% positive). Qualitative: 25 respondents (12 self, 7 client, 6 support); 60% improvement, 5 report no effect, rhythm split. YouTube: 4 views. Drift high (0.627). No AutoGluon elevation. Panel: L1/L2 remain stronger ASCENT candidates; L3 may need redesign.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most listeners feel noticeably calmer within ten minutes, and the rhythm feels comfortable. It works well but not enough people have found it yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: A steady option for anxiety — some feel better quickly, others take longer. Reactions to the music are mixed.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: People report solid improvement, though it takes a bit longer to settle in. The rhythm feels very calming.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Even in crisis moments, many feel significant relief within minutes. The rhythm feels balancing and the music is well-liked.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Quick relief is common and the rhythm feels very settling. Music opinions vary — some love it, others find it just okay.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Fastest settle of the crisis protocols — many feel better almost immediately. Music taste splits sharply though.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: The most reliable overstimulation protocol — strong improvement, calming rhythm, music loved. Still flying under the radar on YouTube.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Works well for overstimulation but listeners may drop off before the full effect kicks in. Music reactions are polarized.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Consistent help for overstimulation, especially when transitioning to sleep or winding down from work. Balanced, calming rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Fast-acting sleep aid — most feel better within five minutes. Music and rhythm both get high marks.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Helps with sleep but takes longer to settle. A few listeners found the rhythm a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Reliable sleep support with balanced rhythm. Works across different listener types — self, caregivers, clients.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Modest creative flow support. Many only feel slight improvement. Rhythm doesn't stand out as impactful.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Stronger creative flow feel — most report meaningful improvement. Half the listeners loved the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Best creative flow scores so far. Compact, consistent results. Music opinions split but rhythm feels balanced.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Lowest focus scores but listeners still report improvement. Some find the rhythm slightly busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Solid focus support with comfortable rhythm. Music reactions mixed but outcomes consistent.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Standout focus protocol — strong improvement, loved music, balanced rhythm. Highest YouTube reach in the series.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Steady maintenance support but takes longer to settle in (around 20 minutes). Rhythm feels balanced.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Mixed results — some feel good, others less so. Rhythm can feel slightly busy for a maintenance protocol.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Promising but early data. Most improve but a subset don't settle at all. Needs more listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mixed mind-clarity results — some report worsening. Rhythm is well-liked but outcomes are inconsistent.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Best mind-clarity protocol so far. Strong improvement reports, very calming rhythm. Still early data.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Lowest scores in the series with high variability. Several listeners found the rhythm too busy. Needs review.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation support shows promise in feedback but scores lag behind. Works fast (5 min settle) but model expects much more.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Dips below L1. Slow settle for some, rhythm slightly busy. Music loved though. Data unstable across runs.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Surprising jump in mean score but high variance and unstable patterns. Early data — watch this one.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Unique 'bridge' signal — model recommends it for five different states. Listeners improve but settle slowly. Highest model prediction.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Nearly identical to L1 with faster settle for some. Reinforces the bridge-protocol idea. Data still stabilizing.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Weaker than L1/L2. Some feel no effect. Rhythm opinions split. L1 and L2 remain the stronger mood-energy options.
